name: Dehydrate after first read (happy path)
trigger: Assistant message reaches finish='stop' on a turn whose parent user message has image attachment_ref parts
preconditions: User message has 1+ attachment_ref parts of mime type image/*
All attachment_refs currently have dehydrated !== true (or undefined)

messages.description: Post-completion hook fires with assistant message id
Read parent user message + its attachment_ref parts
Session.updatePart: set dehydrated=true + annotation. repo_path / sha256 unchanged. (No binary move — binary already at <worktree>/<repo_path> via repo-incoming-attachments upload path.)
Emit telemetry: attachment.dehydrated { sessionID, filename, sha256, originalEstTokens, annotationChars }

postconditions: Each image attachment_ref now has dehydrated=true with annotation populated
Session sqlite attachments table has one fewer binary row per dehydrated image
Next LLM call wire payload will contain <dehydrated_attachment> text block instead of image_url
_recalibrationNote_v2_2026-05-04: P1 messages MSG5-MSG8 simplified — no binary move; binary already at <worktree>/<repo_path>

name: Reread fetches binary back from staging
preconditions: Some prior turn has dehydrated an attachment with that filename
Binary file at ~/.local/state/opencode/incoming/<sid>/<filename> still exists (within TTL)

messages.description: Tool result: { type: 'image', url: 'data:image/png;base64,...', est_tokens, byte_size }

postconditions: Tool result is appended to conversation as a tool-result part containing the image
Next LLM call sees the image binary again (one-shot rehydration via tool channel)

name: Reread fails after user removed file from repo
trigger: Model calls reread_attachment after user has removed the file from <worktree>/incoming/
preconditions: Some prior turn dehydrated this attachment
User removed the file from <worktree>/<repo_path> (git clean / rm)

messages.description: Tool result: { error: 'attachment_not_found', message: 'Image is no longer at <worktree>/<repo_path>. Ask user to re-upload.' }

postconditions: Model receives clear error message and can request re-upload from user

name: Subsequent LLM call serializes dehydrated stub
trigger: Next turn's LLM.stream prepares messages[] including a dehydrated attachment_ref part
preconditions: Conversation history contains at least one attachment_ref with dehydrated=true

messages.description: For each part: if attachment_ref + dehydrated=true → emit text block <dehydrated_attachment filename='X' sha256='Y'>annotation</dehydrated_attachment>; else emit image_url block as today
Returns ModelMessage[] with dehydrated attachments as text blocks
Wire payload contains text annotation, NOT image binary; input_tokens drops accordingly

postconditions: Provider receives dramatically smaller payload for dehydrated attachments
Token usage drops from ~20K per image to ~200-1000 per annotation
